Summary: A new California bill, Assembly Bill 2071, aims to teach students digital wellness in health classes, focusing on responsible technology use rather than outright bans. This initiative is driven by student concerns about mental health impacts from social media and AI, and follows legal actions against tech companies. The bill proposes that the California Department of Education develop a plan by 2028 to educate students on healthy screen habits, algorithms, and online safety. The article highlights the involvement of students like Elise Choi and experts like Kelly Mendoza, and notes the bill's potential to address issues like cyberbullying and promote equitable access to digital wellness education, especially for low-income communities.

The 17th annual Noyo Food Forest Earth Day Festival in Fort Bragg, CA, focused on environmental education and sustainability. The event featured over 50 organizations and vendors, including C&S Waste Solutions promoting recycling and Mendocino Spartan 4-H engaging youth. Noyo Food Forest, in its 20th year, provides hands-on training for students, grows produce for the school cafeteria, and offers internships. The festival also included performances by Mendocino Center for Circus Arts and demonstrations of solar cooking.

The Mendocino Voice, a nonprofit news site, is seeking donations to support local journalism in Mendocino County, CA. A key story highlights Fort Bragg police asking for public assistance in locating a missing 78-year-old man, Philip Cardman. The site also offers a newsletter for updates and mentions various community events and local issues relevant to residents.

The Flock Together Festival, held at Ridgewood Ranch in Willits, CA, celebrates sheep, farming, and agrarian culture with activities like sheep shearing, sheep dog demonstrations, and a ranch-to-table lunch. The event aims to connect people with agriculture by offering hands-on experiences and showcasing the daily work of ranchers. It is an all-ages event with an entry fee of $5, and lunch options are also available for purchase. The festival is supported by The Mendocino Voice, a nonprofit news organization serving Mendocino County, and encourages community involvement and support for local journalism.

The Mendocino Voice reports on the apprehension of a 16-year-old male juvenile suspected of fatally shooting a 38-year-old woman in Covelo. The suspect is also linked to a domestic violence incident involving a 15-year-old female victim, who is a relative of the homicide victim. The article highlights the importance of local journalism and encourages community support for The Mendocino Voice. It also mentions law enforcement agencies, prosecutors, and the general public as key social groups involved.

The Mendocino Voice, an independent nonprofit news site, focuses on local stories for Mendocino County, California. This week's featured adoptable pet is Remi Madea, a Cane Corso available through the Ukiah Animal Shelter, who requires an experienced owner. The publication encourages community support through donations and newsletter subscriptions to sustain its journalism. The content mentions potential adopters, dog owners, and residents of Mendocino County.

The Mendocino Voice reports on the arrest of Raymond Bowes, a Covelo man, on suspicion of burglary and robbery. The incident involved an alleged assault and theft from a male victim, and a woman who was initially reported as kidnapped was found unharmed. Bowes, who was out on bail for a previous felony case, was apprehended and faces additional charges. The article also highlights the importance of local journalism and encourages reader support for The Mendocino Voice.

The Mendocino Voice is a nonprofit news site focused on Mendocino County, California, providing local stories and updates. Key topics include local politics, environmental issues, community events, and crime. The site mentions various social groups such as students, LGBTQ youth, and tribal communities. It also highlights local businesses and agricultural efforts within the county.
